reasoning: Task appears to require producing a concise summary of the provided sports report, capturing the main results, current standings, and upcoming updates.
output: Olympic champion Jessica Ennis-Hill, 29, was third after day one of the Gotzis Hypo-Meeting with 3,928 points. On Sunday she posted a 6.16m long jump and a 42.60m javelin, leaving her with 5,544 points and the 800m still to run, aiming to reach the 6,200 Olympic standard. This is her first heptathlon since winning gold at London 2012. A top-12 finish and 6,075 points would also secure World Championships qualification. Leader Brianne Theisen-Eaton leads with 5,834 points after three personal bests. Updates on day two are on BBC Sport on Sunday, 31 May.
